title: Utilities Due to Report Q3 Earnings on Nov 1: NI, ETR & More
text: Let's focus on utilities like NI, ETR, AWK, DTE and EIX, scheduled to release their third-quarter earnings on Nov 1.

title: Edison International (EIX) to Post Q3 Earnings: What's in Store?
text: Edison International's (EIX) Q3 results are likely to reflect mixed weather pattern impact along with higher interest rate expenses.

title: The 3 Most Undervalued Renewable Energy Stocks to Buy: October 2023
text: Renewable energy stocks are in a slump. An ongoing emphasis on common sense sustainability, interest rates, and increased bond yields are putting pressure on the most promising long-term renewable energy stocks.

title: The DIY EV Portfolio: 7 Stocks From Ground to Garage
text: You may not realize it based on mainstream market reporting the past few years, but  EV stocks are more than a handful of high-risk speculative plays riding Tesla's (NASDAQ: TSLA ) coattails. Instead, if you're bullish on the EV industry – as you should be, despite broader economic effects putting pressure on EV stocks – you should take a step back and consider the entire EV value chain.

title: Edison International: Similarities To Vistra, With Important Differences (Buy Rating Maintained)
text: Edison International is investing heavily in transmission line upgrades to greatly lessen the likelihood of recurrence of past wildfire events attributed to sparks from transmission lines. These capital expenditures are able to be included in Edison International tariff calculations and therefore will contribute an increase in future revenues. The company is mostly financing these capital expenditures with increased net debt, increasing financial risk, offset by reduced exposure to catastrophic risks, and increased revenues from an increased rate base.

title: Edison International: Recession-Resistant Utility With Attractive Dividend Yield
text: Edison International is a regulated electric utility primarily serving Southern California, with stable cash flows and a high yield of 4.66%. While Edison International stock has declined 1.57% year-to-date, it has performed better than the U.S. Utility Index, which has declined 16.32%. The company's revenues have shown stability and resistance to economic shocks, making it an attractive investment option in uncertain times.

title: Edison International Commences Tender Offers to Purchase Its 5.00% Fixed-Rate Reset Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series B and 5.
text: ROSEMEAD, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--  Edison International Commences Tender Offers to Purchase Its 5.00% Fixed-Rate Reset Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series B and 5.375% Fixed-Rate Reset Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series A For a Maximum Aggregate Purchase Price in Cash of Up to $750 Million   Edison International (NYSE: EIX) today announced the commencement of cash tender offers to purchase its outstanding 5.00% Fixed-Rate Reset Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series B.

title: Q4 Stock Predictions: 3 Utility Stocks Ready to Roar Into 2024
text: Investors really should take a hard look at utilities stocks at the moment. Their prices are falling because bond yields are very high.

title: Advisory for Wednesday, Nov. 1: Edison International to Hold Conference Call on Third Quarter 2023 Financial Results

title: EONGY vs. EIX: Which Stock Should Value Investors Buy Now?
text: Investors interested in Utility - Electric Power stocks are likely familiar with E.ON SE (EONGY) and Edison International (EIX). But which of these two stocks offers value investors a better bang for their buck right now?
